claim: For 4096x1024 FP32 LayerNorm on AMD Radeon RX 9070 XT gfx1201, the one-block-per-row shared-memory reduction seed measured 4.945722x faster than the one-thread-per-row baseline using HIP-event timing only.
expected_profiler_signals: more parallel mean and variance reductions
higher effective bandwidth for gamma, beta, and activation reads
curation_note: timing-only LayerNorm record: HIP-event timings and CPU-reference correctness passed, but rocprofiler/rocprof counters were not collected because rocprof, rocprofv3, rocminfo, and rocm-smi were unavailable in this Windows venv environment.
